title: Service Layer
objectives: Distinguish services from repositories and use cases

sections.markdown: A Service is a reusable, focused unit of behavior that isn't domain data itself — analytics, notifications, auth, sync, connectivity. Services are often stateless helpers injected wherever they're needed.

The distinction interviewers love: a **Repository** is about DATA of an aggregate; a **Service** is about a CAPABILITY that doesn't fit one aggregate (auth, notifications, crash reporting). Use cases orchestrate both to perform actions.
Turning every class into a 'Service' — a giant `UserService` doing auth, profile, roles AND messaging is just a God object. Split by capability and cohesion.

flashcards.back: Repository = data access to an aggregate. Service = a capability/integration (auth, analytics, notifications).
No — prefer stateless services; caches/state belong in repositories or dedicated stores.

flashcards.front: Why not one giant Service class?
flashcards.back: It becomes a God object; split by cohesion (one capability per service).

quiz.q: Which is most likely a Service?
quiz.explain: Analytics is a cross-cutting capability, not aggregate data.
Small, focused, testable helpers work best.
Use cases coordinate collaborators to perform one business action.

challenges.text: Extract analytics calls from widgets into an AnalyticsService.
Build an AuthService with signIn/signOut used by multiple screens.
Split a God 'UserService' into AuthService, ProfileRepository, RolesService.
Add a connectivity Service and use it to gate a sync worker.

interviewQuestions: When would a service be stateful?
How do you test a service?
Cross-cutting concerns you'd model as services?
